docs: https://developers.travelperk.com/docs (canonical docs hub; machine-readable OpenAPI 3.1 fragments served per-endpoint at https://developers.perk.com/reference/<slug>.md, confirmed reachable during this review)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: TravelPerk's own developer docs hub (developers.travelperk.com, migrated under the developers.perk.com rebrand) publishes a machine-readable OpenAPI 3.1 fragment per endpoint, fetched directly during this review. The documented surface spans THREE separate hosts with three separate OpenAPI specs: (1) api.travelperk.com (spec title travelperk-api-v1, v1.5) -- trips, invoices, invoice lines, invoice profiles, webhooks; this bundle's spec.json base_url and the ONLY host legacy ever targeted; (2) api.perk.com Cost Centers API (spec title 'Cost Centers API', v1.6) -- a genuinely distinct host/spec version; (3) app.perk.com/api/v2/scim (spec title scim-server) -- SCIM user provisioning, its own auth/versioning model entirely. This bundle covers every GET list endpoint on host (1) as a stream (trips, invoices, plus 2 new: invoice_lines, invoice_profiles) and one new fan_out sub-resource stream (trip_custom_fields). Every endpoint on hosts (2) and (3) is excluded out_of_scope: this bundle's spec.json declares exactly one base_url/one credential, matching legacy's single-host design; adding a second host+auth pair for Cost Centers or SCIM is a scope expansion legacy itself never attempted and is out of this pass's remit. Detail-by-id GETs that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape are excluded duplicate_of; the PDF download is binary_payload. The prior version of this file's 'GET /users'/'GET /expenses' entries were unverified guesses -- neither is a real documented endpoint on any of the three specs above (there is no standalone REST /expenses resource; user data lives entirely under the out-of-scope SCIM host) -- both are removed and replaced with the real, doc-confirmed surface below.

excluded.reason: single-trip detail by id; identical record shape to the trips list stream's per-item object (confirmed from the OpenAPI examples for both endpoints), already covered by that stream
single-invoice detail by serial_number; identical record shape to the invoices list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
returns a rendered PDF document (text/plain octet response per the OpenAPI spec), not JSON record data

registers an outbound event-delivery URL of the caller's choosing; deferred pending its own dedicated security review, consistent with this dialect's general caution around webhook-URL mutations (see persona's/bitly's identically-reasoned webhook exclusions)
repoints an existing outbound event-delivery URL; same webhook-URL-mutation caution as webhook creation

not a documented endpoint: TravelPerk's published OpenAPI surface has no POST /trips (trip creation happens through TravelPerk's own booking UI/flow, not this REST API); legacy never implemented it either

documented on a genuinely distinct host (https://api.perk.com, spec 'Cost Centers API' v1.6) from this bundle's single base_url (https://api.travelperk.com); adding a second host+credential pair is a scope expansion legacy's single-host design never attempted

SCIM user provisioning lives on a completely separate host (app.perk.com/api/v2/scim or app.travelperk.com/api/v2/scim) with its own SCIM 2.0 auth/versioning model, distinct from this bundle's single base_url/api_key; the public docs explicitly note the SCIM API 'differs from Perk's other APIs'
